Planning Your 3-Day LA Itinerary.question: Can you do a 3-day LA solo itinerary without a car?
Planning Your 3-Day LA Itinerary.answer: You can, but it limits you. The Metro connects Hollywood, Downtown, and Santa Monica (Expo Line runs to the beach), and rideshare fills the gaps. The honest constraint: Griffith Observatory, the Getty Center, and canyon day trips are difficult without a car or a $20+ Uber each way. If you're not renting a car, cluster your days by area — a Hollywood day, a Santa Monica/Venice day, a Downtown/Arts District day — to minimize transit time.

Planning Your 3-Day LA Itinerary.question: What's the best day to visit Griffith Observatory solo?
Planning Your 3-Day LA Itinerary.answer: Tuesday through Thursday, arriving before 11am. The Observatory is free and has the best city views in LA, but the parking lot fills by noon on weekends and closes entirely during peak hours. On weekdays the lot stays manageable. The 1.5-mile trail from the Greek Theatre parking lot is a good solo hike up if you want to skip the parking scramble entirely.

Planning Your 3-Day LA Itinerary.question: Is a Venice Beach and Santa Monica day worth doing as a solo traveler?
Planning Your 3-Day LA Itinerary.answer: Yes — it's one of the few LA days that works well entirely on foot. Rent a bike at the Santa Monica Pier ($25/hr) and ride south along the flat coastal path to Venice Boardwalk. The route takes about 20 minutes cycling and you can stop wherever you want. Solo-friendly lunch options are everywhere along the path. Plan 5–6 hours for the combined Santa Monica and Venice leg.

Solo Budget & Logistics.question: What's a realistic daily budget for solo travel in LA?
Solo Budget & Logistics.answer: Budget $170–$260/day mid-range. Hotel: $130–$200/night (cheaper in Hollywood or Koreatown than Santa Monica or West Hollywood). Transport: $20–$40/day depending on Metro vs. rideshare. Meals: $12–$18 lunch, $30–$50 dinner. The Getty Center is free (parking $20). Universal Studios runs $109–$189. Free days (Griffith Observatory, beaches, The Broad) help balance days with paid attractions.

Solo Budget & Logistics.question: Where should a solo traveler stay for a 3-day LA trip?
Solo Budget & Logistics.answer: Hollywood or Koreatown offer the best price-to-location ratio ($110–$160/night). Both are on Metro lines, central to most itinerary stops, and have strong solo dining nearby. Santa Monica is walkable and pleasant but costs $60–$80 more per night and adds travel time to Hollywood and Downtown. Avoid Downtown LA for a leisure solo trip — it requires a ride to most tourist areas regardless.

Solo Budget & Logistics.question: How far in advance should a solo traveler book LA attractions?
Solo Budget & Logistics.answer: The Getty Center requires a free timed-entry reservation — book 1–2 weeks ahead for weekends. Universal Studios tickets are cheapest booked 2+ weeks in advance. The Warner Bros. Studio Tour sells out on weekends; book 1 week ahead. Griffith Observatory, Venice Beach, and the Hollywood Walk of Fame need no booking. For restaurants solo, most places can seat one at the bar same-day — call ahead for weekend dinners at popular spots.
